denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 03/21/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103302223          Group #: GRP-1417
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-161150
Claim #: CLM-2026-210102
Date of Service (requested): 03/01/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners
 Dear Plan Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/03/2026 for Broström ligament repair related to Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Broström ligament repair
Diagnosis (as submitted): Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service. Clinical policy applied: CPB 0117 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0117 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Broström ligament repair is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103302223) and reference PA-2026-161150. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
 Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Broström ligament repair recommended as the next appropriate step. Repeated inversion injuries; MRI ATFL attenuation. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.
